01 — EDA: SMB Underwriting Challenge¶

Intuit TechWeek NYC 2026. Thin narrative layer over src/eda.py (all logic lives there, fully reproducible via python -m src.eda). Organized around the five structural issues that decide this challenge.

See reports/eda_findings.md for the written conclusions; this notebook renders the numbers and figures.

In [1]:
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "..")))
import warnings; warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")
import pandas as pd, numpy as np
from IPython.display import Image, display
from src import data, eda
from src.config import PATHS, PRODUCT, set_seeds
set_seeds()
pd.set_option("display.width", 140); pd.set_option("display.max_columns", 40)
dfs = data.load_all(); tr, va, te = dfs["train"], dfs["validation"], dfs["test"]
print({k: v.shape for k, v in dfs.items()})

{'train': (85340, 44), 'validation': (4489, 44), 'test': (8817, 44)}

0. The split that frames everything — forward-in-time, selection-conditioned labels¶

In [2]:
print("label coverage:"); display(pd.DataFrame(eda.label_coverage(dfs)).T)
print("timestamp ranges + cohort coverage:"); display(pd.DataFrame(eda.timestamp_ranges(dfs)).T)

label coverage:
n labeled labeled_frac default_rate_labeled
train 85340.0 51722.0 0.6061 0.1745
validation 4489.0 2551.0 0.5683 0.2062
test 8817.0 0.0 0.0000 NaN 
timestamp ranges + cohort coverage:
min max cohort_coverage
train 2024-01-01 00:00:00 2025-06-29 00:00:00 0.0
validation 2025-06-30 00:00:00 2025-09-28 00:00:00 1.0
test 2025-06-30 00:00:00 2025-09-28 00:00:00 1.0

1.1 Selection bias / reject inference — the central issue¶

In [3]:
print("prior_decision x observation_status (train):"); display(eda.prior_decision_crosstab(tr))
print("\nApproval rule on prior_underwriter_score (sharp vs fuzzy):")
display(pd.Series(eda.prior_score_threshold(tr)))

prior_decision x observation_status (train):
observation_status matured NaN
prior_decision
0 0 33618
1 51722 0 
Approval rule on prior_underwriter_score (sharp vs fuzzy):

appr_score_mean                0.781
decl_score_mean                0.073
appr_score_min                 0.273
appr_score_max                 1.000
decl_score_min                 0.000
decl_score_max                 0.273
overlap_frac                   0.000
ks                             1.000
auc_score_predicts_approval    1.000
dtype: float64
In [4]:
print("Approved vs declined — top standardized differences:")
display(eda.approved_vs_declined_smd(tr).head(15))

Approved vs declined — top standardized differences:
feature kind smd ks_stat ks_p appr_mean decl_mean abs_effect
0 prior_underwriter_score num 4.176 1.000 0.0 0.781 0.073 4.176
1 prior_decision cat NaN 1.000 0.0 NaN NaN 1.000
2 aggregate_credit_utilization num -0.706 0.275 0.0 0.439 0.597 0.706
3 invoice_payment_delinquency_rate num -0.675 0.277 0.0 0.178 0.273 0.675
4 observed_cash_balance_p10 num 0.668 0.265 0.0 1165.128 -757.147 0.668
5 owner_personal_credit_band num 0.604 0.240 0.0 2.312 1.496 0.604
6 requested_amount num -0.509 0.198 0.0 23724.462 26768.364 0.509
7 bookkeeping_recency_days num -0.400 0.171 0.0 21.112 25.296 0.400
8 payroll_regularity_score num 0.375 0.147 0.0 0.540 0.468 0.375
9 vintage_years num 0.336 0.130 0.0 6.507 5.142 0.336
10 observed_revenue_volatility num -0.320 0.142 0.0 1.058 1.263 0.320
11 stated_time_in_business num 0.308 0.119 0.0 6.891 5.648 0.308
12 account_age_days num 0.292 0.113 0.0 1223.693 986.982 0.292
13 owner_personal_credit_band cat NaN 0.283 0.0 NaN NaN 0.283
14 requested_amount_to_observed_revenue num -0.279 0.118 0.0 0.014 0.016 0.279
In [5]:
display(Image(str(PATHS.reports/"figures"/"fig01_prior_score_threshold.png")))

1.2 Censoring & timing (drives Deliverable B)¶

In [6]:
print("timing structure:"); display(pd.Series(eda.timing_structure(tr)))
haz = eda.discrete_hazard(tr); print("\npooled hazard (head):"); display(haz.head(8))

timing structure:

n_labeled                                  51722
n_default                                   9024
dtd_min                                      3.0
dtd_max                                     90.0
dtd_mean                                    43.1
dtd_median                                  37.0
dtd_pre60_frac                             0.775
dtd_post60_frac                            0.225
obs_status_values                      [matured]
repay_status_values    [paid_in_full, defaulted]
repay_min                                   60.0
repay_max                                   60.0
repay_mean                                  60.0
dtype: object
pooled hazard (head):
day at_risk defaults payoffs hazard cum_default_frac
0 1 51722 0 0 0.00000 0.00000
1 2 51722 0 0 0.00000 0.00000
2 3 51722 123 0 0.00238 0.00238
3 4 51599 112 0 0.00217 0.00454
4 5 51487 142 0 0.00276 0.00729
5 6 51345 139 0 0.00271 0.00998
6 7 51206 135 0 0.00264 0.01259
7 8 51071 152 0 0.00298 0.01553

display(Image(str(PATHS.reports/"figures"/"fig02_default_timing_hazard.png")))
display(Image(str(PATHS.reports/"figures"/"fig03_cumulative_trajectory.png")))

display(Image(str(PATHS.reports/"figures"/"fig04_cohort_default_rate.png")))

cohort_week n_labeled default_rate

12 13 157 0.1529

1.3 Informative missingness — null is signal¶

In [9]:
print("default rate split by null-ness:"); display(eda.missingness_signal(tr))
print("\nnull % by split (top):"); display(eda.missingness_by_split(dfs).head(12))
display(Image(str(PATHS.reports/"figures"/"fig05_missingness_heatmap.png")))

default rate split by null-ness:
feature group n default_rate
0 <<baseline>> all 51722 0.1745
1 has_linked_bank_feed True 34751 0.1665
2 has_linked_bank_feed False 16971 0.1907
3 days_since_last_external_decline null 28334 0.1428
4 days_since_last_external_decline present 23388 0.2128
5 days_since_last_inquiry_elsewhere null 28164 0.1415
6 days_since_last_inquiry_elsewhere present 23558 0.2139
7 prior_approved_amount present 51722 0.1745
8 observed_monthly_revenue_avg_3mo null 16971 0.1907
9 observed_monthly_revenue_avg_3mo present 34751 0.1665 
null % by split (top):
feature null_train null_validation null_test
0 days_since_last_external_decline 0.5019 0.4968 0.4904
1 days_since_last_inquiry_elsewhere 0.4992 0.4888 0.4897
2 prior_approved_amount 0.3939 0.4317 0.4370
3 observed_monthly_revenue_avg_3mo 0.3568 0.3658 0.3674
4 payroll_regularity_score 0.3568 0.3658 0.3674
5 observed_overdraft_count_3mo 0.3568 0.3658 0.3674
6 observed_cash_balance_p10 0.3568 0.3658 0.3674
7 observed_revenue_volatility 0.3568 0.3658 0.3674
8 observed_revenue_trend_3mo 0.3568 0.3658 0.3674
9 application_channel 0.0000 0.0000 0.0000
10 bookkeeping_recency_days 0.0000 0.0000 0.0000
11 multi_lender_inquiry_count_30d 0.0000 0.0000 0.0000

1.4 Self-reported vs observed discrepancies (misreporting signal)¶

In [10]:
print("revenue/tenure discrepancy:"); display(pd.Series(eda.revenue_discrepancy(tr)))
print("\nprovided requested_amount_to_observed_revenue sanity-check:")
display(pd.Series(eda.recompute_ratio_check(tr)))
display(Image(str(PATHS.reports/"figures"/"fig06_revenue_discrepancy.png")))

revenue/tenure discrepancy:

n_with_feed                   34751.0000
ratio_median                      0.9490
ratio_p10                         0.7180
ratio_p90                         1.2690
frac_overstate_1.5x               0.0240
default_rate_overstate            0.3859
default_rate_not_overstate        0.1611
tib_gap_median                    0.2720
tib_stated_gt_vintage_frac        0.3290
dtype: float64
provided requested_amount_to_observed_revenue sanity-check:

provided_null_frac                     0.000000
provided_null_when_no_feed_frac        0.000000
n_comparable                       54887.000000
max_abs_diff_vs_naive_recompute        1.122600
median_abs_diff                        0.137001
dtype: float64

1.5 Entity & leakage structure¶

In [11]:
print("entity structure:"); display(pd.Series(eda.entity_structure(dfs)))
print("\nleakage canary — single-feature AUC for default (top):"); display(eda.leakage_audit(tr).head(12))

entity structure:

business_overlap_tr_va             0.000
business_overlap_tr_te             0.000
business_overlap_va_te             0.000
train_n_businesses             68364.000
train_multi_app_businesses     14425.000
train_max_apps_per_business        4.000
repeat_app_count_max               3.000
prior_loans_count_max              6.000
frac_first_time                    0.676
dtype: float64
leakage canary — single-feature AUC for default (top):
feature auc raw_auc
0 invoice_payment_delinquency_rate 0.7506 0.7506
1 aggregate_credit_utilization 0.7353 0.7353
2 observed_cash_balance_p10 0.6789 0.3211
3 requested_amount_to_observed_revenue 0.6675 0.6675
4 requested_amount 0.6491 0.6491
5 prior_approved_amount 0.6402 0.6402
6 owner_personal_credit_band 0.6189 0.3811
7 payroll_regularity_score 0.6114 0.3886
8 observed_revenue_volatility 0.6082 0.6082
9 existing_debt_obligations 0.6066 0.6066
10 prior_underwriter_score 0.5868 0.4132
11 bookkeeping_recency_days 0.5860 0.5860
In [12]:
import json; print("class balance (default rate by group):")
cb = eda.class_balance(tr, ["owner_personal_credit_band","sector","application_channel","geography_region"])
print(json.dumps(cb, indent=2)[:1200])

class balance (default rate by group):
{
  "overall": 0.1745,

2. Boring-but-necessary checks¶

In [13]:
import json; print(json.dumps(eda.boring_checks(dfs), indent=2, default=str)[:1500])

{
  "constant_cols": [],
  "duplicate_rows_excl_id": 0,
  "dtype_mismatches": [
    {
      "field": "default_flag",
      "dict": "bool",
      "actual": "float64"
    }
  ],
  "outliers": {
    "cash_balance_p10_negative_frac": 0.2858,
    "requested_amount_min": 7590.5,
    "requested_amount_max": 45902.5,
    "requested_amount_out_of_5k_50k_frac": 0.0,
    "existing_debt_p99": 27021.2,
    "existing_debt_max": 69238.7
  },
  "categorical_cardinality": {
    "sector": 5,
    "geography_region": 4,
    "employee_count_bucket": 4,
    "intended_use_of_funds": 4,
    "owner_personal_credit_band": 5,
    "application_channel": 3,
    "prior_decision": 2
  },
  "prior_decision_values": [
    0,
    1
  ]
}

3. Deliverable C — intervention-query support¶

In [14]:
s = eda.intervention_support(dfs)
print("queries:", s["n_queries"], "applicants:", s["n_applicants"], "all in test:", bool(s["all_applicants_in_test"]))
print("queried but dict says NOT intervenable:"); print(s["queried_not_dict_intervenable"])
print("\nout-of-support interventions (frac of query values in 1-99 pctile):")
sup = pd.DataFrame(s["support"]).T.sort_values("frac_in_1_99_support")
display(sup[sup["frac_in_1_99_support"] < 0.8])

queries: 900 applicants: 300 all in test: True
queried but dict says NOT intervenable:
['account_age_days', 'bookkeeping_recency_days', 'days_since_last_external_decline', 'days_since_last_inquiry_elsewhere', 'employee_count_bucket', 'geography_region', 'has_linked_bank_feed', 'intended_use_of_funds', 'platform_active_months', 'prior_loans_amount_total', 'prior_loans_count', 'prior_loans_default_count', 'sector', 'vintage_years']

out-of-support interventions (frac of query values in 1-99 pctile):
n_queries frac_in_1_99_support
prior_loans_count 7.0 0.286
recent_inquiries_count_6mo 49.0 0.449
platform_active_months 19.0 0.579
prior_loans_default_count 13.0 0.615
observed_overdraft_count_3mo 47.0 0.638
multi_lender_inquiry_count_30d 34.0 0.647
observed_revenue_trend_3mo 53.0 0.717
